name: Plas Johnson
abstract: Plas John Johnson Jr. () (born July 21, 1931) is an American soul-jazz and hard bop tenor saxophonist, probably most widely known as the tenor saxophone soloist on Henry Mancini’s "The Pink Panther Theme".

interviews.title: Plas Johnson interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 12, 1996

concerts.label: Carnegie Hall Jazz Band: Saxophone Summit

about_quotes.speaker: Clarence Ford
about_quotes.evidence: And the next band, one of them was the Johnson Brothers, they called me. ... I worked with them. ... Plas Johnson is right now in Hollywood, he plays tenor. ... I was playing alto sax and they had a five piece band ... I think that's the first record I made

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Scott
about_quotes.evidence: I got Plas Johnson; Jackie Kelso, another wonderful saxophone player; Bill Perkins, another saxophone player... And Plas Johnson also.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom Scott interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, April 1, 2022 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Chuck Findley
about_quotes.evidence: You heard Plas Johnson you knew it was Plas Johnson. I listened to everybody and I picked up everybody from playing records and records and sitting next to them and playing and picking up their little innuendoes
about_quotes.doc_title: Chuck Findley interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, September 6, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Ernie Fields
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Look at this pictures and since we're going to get to that In The Mood thing, there's Plas Johnson... [INTERVIEWEE] Oh, yeah.

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: Palmer drums, Plas Johnson, saxophone. And Frank Assolino, Snooky Young. ... we did this tour as a result of the album.

about_quotes.speaker: John Altman
about_quotes.evidence: when you’ve got Pete Christlieb or Plas Johnson or Bob Efford sitting there... I had a big band in L.A.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Altman interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, September 14, 2023
